steps.title: Spot the structure
steps.content: The blank contains a dependent clause introduced by “where” that modifies “mouthpiece.” This type of clause uses normal word order (subject before verb), not inverted word order like a question.

steps.title: Match the correct form
steps.content: Choice C is correct with “where sound is made.”—normal word order ending with a period. B and D both invert to “where is sound made,” and A ends with a question mark—none of the three alternatives meet the requirements for a complete sentence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
